Candidate name Amarnath Sm Job Title Sr. Software Engineer - Fullstack Date Friday, February 27th, 2026 Assessment Interview score 8 OUT OF 10 AI Summary Technical Skill 4 / 5 Problem Solving 3 / 5 Code Quality 3 / 5 Communication Rating 4 / 5 Interview Summary Language/Framework… 4 Software Engineering 3 Frontend Concepts 3 8 OUT OF 10

HIGHLIGHTS The candidate demonstrates strong backend fundamentals in C#/.NET along with working knowledge of React and frontend concepts. Shows practical understanding of concurrency concepts such as thread pool starvation, async blocking calls, ConcurrentDictionary, and Interlocked. Has hands-on experience in API design, versioning, pagination, idempotency concepts, and cloud exposure (S3, Lambda, Terraform deployments). Some gaps remain in distributed system concepts (API Gateway, blue-green deployment, optimistic vs pessimistic locking), but overall performance aligns well with expectations for a strong IC fullstack engineer. Suitable for Senior Software Engineer – Fullstack role. Highlights • Good explanation of thread pool starvation scenarios (.Wait/.Result causing blocking). • Understands non-blocking async and importance of await

. • Understands non-blocking async and importance of await. • Strong grasp of ConcurrentDictionary and shared resource updates. • Knows usage of Interlocked for atomic operations. • Correct understanding of idempotency and API contracts(high level). • Able to explain pagination design clearly. • Good React fundamentals (re-render triggers, Virtual DOM, memoization). • Exposure to AWS hosting, Terraform, Jenkins deployments. SCOPE OF IMPROVEMENT • Could not clearly differentiate race condition vs deadlock. • Async deadlock explanation lacked depth (focused mostly on sync context). • Blue-green deployment unfamiliar. • API Gateway explanation missing. • Locking strategies (optimistic vs pessimistic) unclear. • Limited clarity on how rate limiting is enforced technically. • Reconciliation explanation in React was partial

. • Limited clarity on how rate limiting is enforced technically. • Reconciliation explanation in React was partial. Language/Framework Fundamentals C# ENTITY FRAMEWORK MVC 4 Solid Performance, Exceeds Expectations OVERALL FEEDBACK • Strong C# fundamentals. • Good understanding of thread pool starvation and blocking calls. • Explained ConcurrentDictionary correctly for concurrent updates. OUT OF 5

• Understood Interlocked usage for atomic increments. • Knows async best practices (avoid blocking calls like .Wait/.Result). • Slight confusion in race condition vs deadlock differentiation. • MVC, WCF, ASP.NET experience . • Testing experience present (though not deeply probed in this round). Overall: Strong backend language fundamentals suitable for senior IC role. Candidate has Good knowledge QUESTION 1 Explain thread pool starvation ConcurrentDictionary - Interlocked - i How do you debug race conditionsfi - deadlock where you cannot recover , example race condition race condition vs deadlock what was the situation - What causes deadlocks in async codefi what is sync code - FEEDBACK Explain thread pool starvation - no more threads in thredpool , Scarios - using asynchronous calls , using blocking calls . .wait or

. .wait or . Result, high performing tasks and all blocking calls , non blocking calss and always await , static values , u can use interlocked , in async operations , dll , don't need to manage execution context ConcurrentDictionary - lock management system , locks for every thread , resources are shared but can be updated concurrently , When should you use it - updating simultaneously , complex Value fi Interlocked - increent static values , value is shared and multiple increase it How do you debug race conditionsfi - deadlock where you cannot recover , 2 objects accessing at same time , example race condition - one using it another is trying to use race condition vs deadlock - not sure what was the situation - What causes deadlocks in async codefi he said deadlock occurs in sync

what is sync code - Software Engineering 3 Performance Meets Expectations OVERALL FEEDBACK REST API design understanding solid. • Correct explanation of PUT vs PATCH. • Good understanding of idempotency concept. • Pagination design explained clearly. • Rate limiting concept known but enforcement mechanics unclear. • Hosting knowledge via IIS and AWS (Terraform + Jenkins). • AWS exposure (S3, Lambda) present but limited hands-on testing clarity. • API Gateway concept not clearly articulated. • Blue-green deployment unfamiliar. • Locking strategy knowledge limited. Overall: Strong application-level engineering, moderate distributed systems depth

. • Locking strategy knowledge limited. Overall: Strong application-level engineering, moderate distributed systems depth. Candidate has Basic knowledge QUESTION 1 REST API - What is the other type , RPCfi PUT vs PATCH differencefi What is idempotencyfi How do you implement API versioningfi what are breaking changes fi What is a API contract How do you design paginationfi How do you implement rate limitingfi how do you host your controllers - how application is hosted in AWS Cloud - implementing S3 , Lambda functions ----- How did it test it fi OUT OF 5

what is an API Gateway ----- What is blue-green deploymentfi Optimistic vs pessimistic lockingfi FEEDBACK REST API - What is the other type , RPCfi PUT vs PATCH differencefi update is PUT , required resources , What is idempotencyfi the same req expects same response POST needs action to make sure idempotency How do you implement API versioningfi - moving to new technology , we need versioning , we need to provide support , what are breaking changes fi if your reqeust and response can break client What is a API contract - type OF Request and model , Request - Data contract , /v1/Controller /ACTioN Methods , POST : SaveScanDetailsAsync , Req = IN payload , you will have attributes , Res : Response Model , Headers : certain endpoints had , How do you design paginationfi he could explain , How do you implement rate limitingfi can set at

Model , Headers : certain endpoints had , How do you design paginationfi he could explain , How do you implement rate limitingfi can set at controller level , how do you make sure , how this is maintianed , how controller level you know how many requests have come , how do you host your controllers - IIS , AWS - Dedicated ops team , how application is hosted in AWS - Terraforms , Jenkins deployed locally , Cloud - implementing S3 , Lambda functions ----- How did it test it fi he created local stack fi Difficult what is an API Gateway ----- What is blue-green deploymentfi dont remember Optimistic vs pessimistic lockingfi Frontend Concepts 3 Performance Meets Expectations OVERALL FEEDBACK • React fundamentals solid (re-render triggers, props/state changes)

. • Virtual DOM explanation correct. • Memoization concept understood (prevent unnecessary re-renders). • Forms handling basic explanation. • Reconciliation explanation incomplete. • TypeScript knowledge present but not deeply tested in this round. OUT OF 5

Overall: Good functional frontend capability suitable for 30% frontend responsibility. Candidate has Basic knowledge QUESTION 1 What causes a React component to re-renderfi What is virtual DOMfi What is reconciliationfi How do you handle forms in Reactfi What is memoization in Reactfi FEEDBACK What causes a React component to re-renderfi - changes in component state , api result , parents prop changes What is virtual DOMfi it hold were done compare and updates , needed as lightweight , updates dom where it si needed , What is reconciliationfi How do you handle forms in Reactfi - reat forms What is memoization in Reactfi - caching your data so that it doesnot rerender everytime , Interview Recording Link Recording link : link
